page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-2 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance configured with 2 vCPUs and 4 GB of system memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor operating at a base frequency of 2.3 GHz, this x86_64 architecture instance provides 1 physical core with 2 threads per core under a dedicated CPU allocation model. The processor features a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache, alongside advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for specialized mathematical operations. Local storage is not bundled with this instance type.

Performance benchmarks reveal a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The instance delivers top-tier single-core performance, ranking in the top 10% for Geekbench single-core scores and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, due to its low core count, multi-core benchmarks such as stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores fall into the bottom 25%. Memory performance is also a notable bottleneck, with uncached large-block read bandwidth and Passmark memory scores landing in the bottom 10%.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-highcpu-2 serves as an entry-level compute-optimized option with a low resource footprint. It is highly efficient for workloads that require strong single-threaded execution but do not demand high memory capacity or parallel processing. Tradeoffs include the lack of local storage and limited memory bandwidth. This instance is best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications, basic text processing, simple web serving, and low-intensity utility scripts.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-2 is a compute-optimized instance configured with 2 vCPUs and 4 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon 8581C processor at 2.3 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides a dedicated CPU allocation with 1 physical core and 2 threads. The instance features a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmark data highlights exceptional single-core performance, placing in the top 10% for Geekbench single-core and single-threaded compression tasks. Conversely, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are limited, scoring in the bottom 25% and 10% respectively. Lacking local storage, this instance represents a low-footprint, cost-efficient option for single-threaded workloads. It is best suited for lightweight compute tasks, basic text processing, and single-threaded utility scripts.
